Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed metaphorical clause (Verb + subject + locative phrase)
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone acting immature despite being old enough to know better
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain Rephrasi: Macaay ko tamdaw a radiw no kapah
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, family correction, mild sarcasm
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Riyar (sea) symbolizes unpredictability in coastal Pangcah communities of Taitung
First Example Sentence: Cangra ko wawa i riyar, saka pakafana' to misanga'ay.
Second Example Sentence: Awaay ka, cangra ko wawa i riyar kiso.
Third Example Sentence: Cangra ko wawa i riyar a radiw no kapah nira.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Stative verb phrase (ma- adjective + oblique object)
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Lalan (road) symbolizes life journey in many Pangcah narratives
First Example Sentence: Malemeday to lalan ko tamdaw a romi'ad to sapalengaw.
Second Example Sentence: Malemeday to lalan nira, saka mafana' to radiw.
Third Example Sentence: Saka malemeday to lalan ko niyaro nira.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Ko'ol (heart) in Pangcah worldview represents emotional core
First Example Sentence: Fana' ko ko'ol nira to sowal no kapah.
Second Example Sentence: Saka fana' ko ko'ol ko wama a masadak.
Third Example Sentence: Caka fana' ko ko'ol, malacecay kita.

First Example Sentence: Pakayni to fafahiyan, saka ma'araw to kapah.
Second Example Sentence: Pakayni to fafahiyan ko tamdaw a romi'ad.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Used in regional political commentary in eastern Taiwan
First Example Sentence: Oroma ko sowal nira, awaay ko misalama.
Third Example Sentence: Oroma ko sowal no tamdaw a radiw.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Riyar frequently symbolizes emotional states in coastal Pangcah culture
First Example Sentence: Mafaca' ko riyar ko radiw nira.
Second Example Sentence: Saka mafaca' ko riyar a ma'araw.
Third Example Sentence: Mafaca' ko riyar to misalama nira.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A person easily influenced by others
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Faloco' (wind) symbolizes instability in oral tradition
First Example Sentence: Radiw no faloco' ko wawa a romi'ad.

Third Example Sentence: Radiw no faloco' kiso saka caka to sowal.
